title: Fancifully Crafted
description: A mod that adds various Fancy Pants related blocks, items and world generation changes that take reference from the Fancy Pants Adventures series.
body:
![Alt text](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/6LV1haeS/images/980f9b039f2964141f89c68f4f93bfdeafde1133.png)

## **What is this mod?**
---

This mod aims to transfer the many ideas and designs of Brad Borne from the flash game "The Fancy Pants Adventures" into the world of Minecraft!

## **What does this mod include?**

**First, this mod includes a custom made HUD**
-
- The HUD keeps track of any players squiggle count.
- Once 1000 Squiggles are collected, the player is awarded with One Life, which takes up an inventory slot. If one prefers not to have lives, they can simply store them away.
- The Pants item is activated regardless of location in inventory.
- If FC is being used on a server, it will announce to all players when any player has obtained 1000 squiggles. If a player collects 10,000 squiggles, a sound is played for all players.
![Alt text](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/6LV1haeS/images/ff6ba7479bda46d7680e4c4adea30745c627979b.png)

**This mod includes roughly 70 items and blocks which are as follows:**
-

- "Deepslate Brad Ore",
Generated in the world, Needs Nethorite.
- "Mosaic of the Bradith",
Block of Brad's Face. He also Speaks. Escucho Voces.
- "Bradium and the Polished Essence Bradium",
The Very Essence of Brad, Needed for many recipes.
Please note: (I have showed this to Brad, he got a good chuckle over it so don't worry about it)
- "Mayor Armor Set",
A little stronger then Netherite, gives off the appearance of Mayor from the hit game Fancy Pants World 2 Remaster.

- "Ice Cream",
May be hard to craft, However There is a Chance it Could be an Upgrade ice cream!
(The random pool is SPEED, JUMP BOOST, STRENGTH, and RESISTANCE, They Can Mix and Match Each Time it is Consumed for Around 30 Seconds.)
- "Mayor Of Undying",
Painfully Hard to Craft, However Beating Up a Warden Gives out 4-6 of them.
The Mayor Of Undying Works like an adrenaline rush, on top of what a normal totem does, on death it gives both Strength III and Haste III for around a minute for either fight or flight gameplay.
- "Ink Blob",
Can be thrown like a snowball, does damage and drops glowing blobs on the floor.
- "Squiggle",
When Right Clicked Heals 1 Heart and increases your squiggle counter.
- "Pants",
Given at 1000 Squiggles. Leave this item in your inventory and it will count as a life, when you die, one is removed and taken away from your HUD and counter.
- "Floating Squiggle",
When Touched (By Any Entity) Heals 1 Heart, increases the players squiggle count and disappears.
- "Floating Green Squiggle",
When Touched (By Any Entity) Heals 5 Hearts, increases the players squiggle count by 5 and disappears.
- "PencilSword",
Base stats include 12 Attack Damage and 0.6 attack Speed, Can be enchanted.
- "Bathtub Lounge",
Disc that can be played in game.
- "World 2 Boss",
Disc that can be played in game.
-  "Mayor's Theme",
Disc that can be played in game.
- "Mystery",
Disc that can be played in game.
- "Spring",
Slime Block on Steroids with many changes such as selectable thrust when right clicked.
- "Sketch Block",
Everything is animated and it glows.
-  "Fancy Ground",
From W1, comes with full blockset and vertical slab
 - "Fancy Tough",
From W1
- "Fancy Grass",
Generated in the world.
- "Scribble Grass",
Generated in the world, Drops Squiggle if broken. (Silk Touch needed)
-  "Fancy Grass Block",
From W2 I think.
- "Fancy Dirt Block"
From W2 I think.
- "Glass...?",
From the W3 opening cutscene, he is the one who knocks.

- "Flowered Cactus",
Crafted from water and Cactus blocks, from W2.

- "Squiggle Potion",
Brewed from a squiggle, gives you a momentary full heal, and can be brewed into a II, splash, and lingering potion along with arrow effects.

- "Painting Variant",
Is a reference to W3's painting.

- "Trimmed Fancy Ground",
From W3, is obtained by using a stone cutter on Fancy Ground and comes with a full blockset.

- "Fancy Wood",
An entire new tree and all the items that come with a normal wood type are included. References include the door from the FPA games along with the blue door from W3!

- "Stripped Fancy Wood",
Another entire woodset craftable from stripped Fancy Wood and looks pretty nice.

- "Fancy Valley",
Spawns in the world in clumps, hard to break, and only spawns y60 to sky limit.

- "Packed Fancy Valley",
Reference to SFPA and Tree Pirate Valley, can be turned into Valley Columns if stripped with an axe, can be undone.

- "Fancy Spike",
Instantly teleports the player and spawns only in Norris Temples (from W3)

- "Harming Spike",
Instantly kills the player and spawns only in Norris Temples (from W3)

- "Norris Temples",
A new structure which is designed to give the player and optional challenge and gives loot from the mod. Can only be found in Taiga biomes.

- "Fancy Chiseled Sandstone",
A new variation to Chiseled Sandstone that has a familiar face on it.

![Alt text](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/6LV1haeS/images/231d422db8decb75b3b534bbedea937935d16082.png)

## **Other features in this mod**
---
Many loot tables in MC have been modified for a smoother experience for this mod in mind: 

- The world naturally contains fancy grass, fancy trees, fancy pops, and Norris temples.

- Every overworld Biome contains many randomized patterns of Squiggles.

- Villagers trade fancy blocks and items matching to their respective profession

- Wandering Traders trade fancy saplings, ink blobs and planks.

- Dungeons have around a 50% chance to drop the "Bathtub Lounge" and "Mystery" discs.

- Fishing Loot can drop some Bradium.

- End City Chests Drop two exclusive dics from the mod.

- Ship Wrecks have a decent amount of ice cream. 
Wonder what that's a reference to…

- Ruined Portals have a low chance to contain Bradium.

- If a Creeper is defeated with The Pencil Sword, it has a low chance to drop Bradium.
![Alt text](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/6LV1haeS/images/f23a4d45f6d59b66dcc12620a5c6fd86913c1a08.png)
